authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service — Eldorado National Forest (Desolation Wilderness)

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Lake Sylvia not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Lake Sylvia not listed)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Desolation Wilderness Permits — Eldorado National Forest, USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.

sources.title: Desolation Day Use Permits & Parking — Eldorado National Forest, USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Day users may obtain a free Day Use permit from the Placerville Ranger Station or may issue themselves a day use permit at most major trailheads in the summer. During the winter, day use permits are not required (roughly October to March depending on weather conditions). Day use is not subject to fees nor limited by quota at any time of the year. Permits are only required when the permit boxes are installed at the trailheads.

Backcountry Fishing in Desolation Wilderness — Sierra Recreation Magazine
sources.quotedText: Travel further in on the Lyons trail towards Pyramid Peak for a little more desolation and Brooke trout fishing.

parameters.note: Lake Sylvia is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Lake Sylvia lies within the Desolation Wilderness (Eldorado National Forest), reached via the Lyons Creek Trail. A wilderness permit is required year-round for overnight use, with a limited quota system in effect from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30.

raw.quota_season: Friday before Memorial Day through September 30

regulations.citation: USFS Eldorado National Forest — Desolation Wilderness Permits
regulations.humanSummary: Wilderness permit required for overnight camping at Lake Sylvia year-round (quota system in effect late May–September)
regulations.verbatimText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.

parameters.description: Day users to Desolation Wilderness (including Lake Sylvia via the Lyons Creek Trail) must self-issue a free day-use permit at trailhead permit boxes, available in summer, or obtain one in person from the Placerville Ranger Station. Day-use permits are not required in winter (roughly October through March) and day use is never subject to fees or a quota.

raw.permit_required: day use, seasonal (self-issue at trailhead boxes in summer; not required roughly October–March)

regulations.citation: USFS Eldorado National Forest — Desolation Day Use Permits & Parking
regulations.humanSummary: Day-use visitors must self-issue a free wilderness permit at trailhead boxes (summer) or from the Placerville Ranger Station; not required roughly October through March
regulations.verbatimText: Day users may obtain a free Day Use permit from the Placerville Ranger Station or may issue themselves a day use permit at most major trailheads in the summer. During the winter, day use permits are not required (roughly October to March depending on weather conditions). Day use is not subject to fees nor limited by quota at any time of the year. Permits are only required when the permit boxes are installed at the trailheads.
